title: sign(): wrong error variant for oversized sig_sz
description: wolftpm/src/key.rs:159-161. After wolfTPM2_SignHashScheme returns success, the check 'if sig_sz < 0 || sig_sz as usize > sig.len()' returns Error::BufferTooSmall. That variant means the caller's buffer was too small before the call. If this branch fires (sig_sz > sig.len() on success return), it means wolfTPM returned a size larger than the allocated buffer after claiming success — that is a protocol violation, not a buffer-size problem. Change the return to Error::UnexpectedResponse to match the established convention (same as pcr_read uses when digest_len \!= 32).
